summary: Frontend-only retest of V4 popup fix on Monitor button (Dispositivi tab). Fix in WebConsoleTabs.js:127 — removed 'noopener,noreferrer' from window.open(absUrl,'_blank') so Chromium returns a valid WindowProxy. ALL acceptance criteria PASS on both desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (390x844): (1) POST /api/console-v4/request-session is called, (2) POST /api/web-console/session (V3) is NOT called, (3) POST /api/admin/wireguard/session/start is called in parallel, (4) data-testid=web-console-active dock is NOT rendered (count=0), (5) no 'Pop-up bloccato' alert in DOM, (6) no ReferenceError in console. The previous V3-fallback regression observed in iteration_61 is fully resolved.
critical_code_review_comments: WebConsoleTabs.js:127 — removing 'noopener,noreferrer' is correct given the V4 proxy serves URLs on the SAME origin (window.location.origin + /api/console-v4/s/<jwt>/), so reverse-tabnabbing risk is null (no third-party page can manipulate window.opener). Acceptable trade-off for distinguishing 'popup blocked' (null) from 'popup opened' (WindowProxy).

WebConsoleTabs.js openPopup — JWT token is still embedded in URL path (/api/console-v4/s/<JWT>/). This is logged by ingress access logs and stays in browser history. Recommend (post-MVP) using opaque session id mapped server-side, or a one-shot redirect that exchanges JWT in path for a HttpOnly cookie.

ClientOverviewPage.js openConsoleWithVpn — the V3 fallback path (webConsole.open()) is still wired as a safety net if openPopup() returns null (real popup-block case). Behavior is correct; only triggers on actual block now.

Pre-existing React warning (LoginPage 'Cannot update a component while rendering a different component') is unrelated to V4 fix — same warning observed across previous iterations. Not blocking but worth cleaning up in LoginPage.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (6/6 critical assertions passed on desktop + mobile)

context_for_next_testing_agent: V4 popup fix verified. On click of [data-testid='web-console-btn-192.168.1.3']: only POST /api/console-v4/request-session + /api/admin/wireguard/session/start fire (no V3 /api/web-console/session). web-console-active dock NOT rendered. No popup-blocked alert. Note: Playwright context.on('page') did not capture the new tab in headless mode (popup target is a backend /api/ URL); however absence of alert + absence of V3 fallback confirms window.open returned a valid WindowProxy (not null). Pre-existing console warning from LoginPage about setState-in-render is unrelated.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Previous iteration_61 bug: window.open(url,'_blank','noopener,noreferrer') returns null in Chromium per spec, which incorrectly triggered the if(!win) popup-blocked path → V3 fallback + dock + alert.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: WebConsoleTabs.js line 127 changed to window.open(absUrl, '_blank') without noopener/noreferrer. Same-origin V4 proxy URL makes reverse-tabnabbing risk negligible.
rca_of_the_issue.verification: Network trace post-fix shows ONLY V4 + WG endpoints, no V3. DOM shows no web-console-active dock, no 'Pop-up bloccato' text. Tested on desktop 1920x1080 and mobile 390x844.
